speaking.sub: Draining the audio channel to one voice.

interrupt.sub: Atomic drop. Queue cleared. Channel flushed.

hero.subtitle: Huxley is a Python framework for real-time voice agents. You bring a persona and skills — it handles turn coordination, interrupts, proactive speech, audio bridging.
hero.installSnippet: git clone huxley && uv run huxley

problem.titleLine1: Every voice platform asks you to
problem.subtitle: Ownership. Extensibility. Or the first six months of your life.

pipecat.name: Pipecat / LiveKit
pipecat.bad2: You solve turns, interrupts, audio collisions

diy.name: Build it yourself
diy.bad1: Six months of plumbing before the first feature

huxley.name: Huxley
huxley.good1: Opinionated on turn sequencing and audio flow
huxley.good2: Open for skills, personas, and clients
huxley.good3: Self-hosted Python — your box, your keys

architecture.subtitle: The framework sequences every turn through a single speaker. Model voice, tool audio, and skill-claimed bridges never collide.

turnCoord.v: Model speech always finishes before tool audio. Interrupts are atomic — drop flag, clear queue, flush, cancel.

skillDispatch.v: Python packages registered via entry-points. Typed context, namespaced storage, no framework internals leaked.

proactive.v: Skills call ctx.inject_turn() and Huxley speaks first — without waiting for the user. Timers, alerts, doorbells, inbound messages.

bridging.v: Skills can claim the mic and speaker for full-duplex audio (calls, voice memos). The focus manager prevents collisions with model speech.

constraints.v: never_say_no, confirm_destructive, child_safe — declared in persona.yaml and enforced system-wide. Build for vulnerable users without special code.

provider.v: OpenAI Realtime today; the architecture leaves room for other providers, but Huxley itself doesn’t train or serve models.

timeline.subtitle: The coordinator drains model speech before tool audio starts, holds proactive turns until the channel is free, and makes every interrupt atomic.

callouts.t2Body: Model finishes its sentence before the chime plays.
callouts.t3Body: Proactive inject waits for the previous turn to drain.

skills.subtitle: Skills register via Python entry-points. Add a line to persona.yaml, restart — the framework never changes.
skills.writingTitle: A skill is a Python package.
skills.writingBody: Declare tools. Handle calls. Return a ToolResult — optionally with an AudioStream, a PlaySound, or an InputClaim for full-duplex audio. The framework sequences the rest.

grows.subtitle: First it checks huxley-market — a registry of free, open-source skills built by other users. If nothing fits, huxley-grows writes one from scratch. Either way, no terminal. Install from voice, or tap it through the Huxley-web app on your phone.

ceremony.v: Market install and build-from-scratch end the same way: a new skill on your box, same API, same permissions model, same voice command.

noTerminal.v: Install from voice, or tap it through huxley-web on your phone. The CLI still works — you just never have to touch it.

failure.v: If neither path works — no market hit and no buildable shape — Huxley says so, precisely, and stops. No hallucinated capability.

personas.subtitle: A persona is a YAML file. Pick the voice, the language, the skills, the guardrails. Huxley adapts — the person you’re building for shouldn’t have to.
